title: Outsourcing
summary: 9609 AS — outsourcing rationale, core competencies, benefits, risks, and evaluation.
learningObjectives: Analyse benefits and risks of outsourcing.
Recommend whether a function should be outsourced.

simpleExplanation.title: Using outside specialists
simpleExplanation.summary: Outsourcing means contracting external firms to perform business activities. Firms focus on core competencies while specialists handle support functions — but control and quality risks rise.
steps: Focus on what the firm does best — core competencies.
Quality control and communication harder at distance.
Dependency on supplier reliability and reputation risk.

flashcards.back: Using external suppliers to perform business functions previously done in-house.
Outsourcing to firms in another country — often lower wages.
Activity where firm has distinctive advantage — keep in-house.
IT support, payroll, cleaning, call centres, component manufacture.
Lower cost, access to expertise, flexibility, focus on core.
Quality failures, data security, job losses, supplier failure.
Shareholders may favour cost cuts; employees/unions oppose job losses (1.5).
Losing manufacturing may lose process knowledge and innovation.

sections.content: **Outsourcing** (4.3.2) — distinguish **what to outsource** (non-core) from **strategic capabilities** (keep). Link job losses to **2.1 HRM** and **stakeholders (1.5)**.

sections.question: UK software firm outsources customer support to overseas call centre. Costs fall 40% but online reviews cite poor English and long wait times. Evaluate the outsourcing decision.
sections.solution:
**Financial benefit:** **40% cost saving** improves margins (10.2 ratios).

**Non-financial cost:** **Brand damage** — reviews affect **new customer acquisition** (3.1.6); may exceed savings.

**Core competency test:** Support for **complex B2B software** may need **in-house product experts** — wrongly treated as commodity.

**Recommendation:** **Reshore tier-2 support** or **hybrid** — outsource simple queries only; measure **NPS** not just cost.
